Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code for Selector block with multidimensional input and variable-size output
Description: The generated code of a Selector block with a multidimensional input might set incorrect dimensions of the resulting variable-dimension signal under all of the following conditions:   The multidimensional input signal includes one dimension of size 1, which is the third or higher dimension. For example, an input signal of size: [4][3][1][2] .   The Selector block parameter, Index Option , is set to Starting and ending indices (port)  on an input signal dimension of size 1. All subsequent dimensions from the dimension of size 1, have an Output Size  of 1.  For example, if the input signal is [3][4][1][4]  and the Selector block parameters are:   Input  1: Index Option  Starting index (dialog) ; Index  1; Output Size  2   Input  2: Index Option  Index vector (dialog) ; Index  1:3;   Input  3: Index Option  Starting and ending indices (port)    Input  4: Index Option  Starting index (dialog) ; Index  2; Output Size  1  The output signal is [2][3][1][1]  but Simulink omits the trailing 1's so that the actual output signal is [2][3] . The generated code is incorrect since all of the above conditions are met.
Workaround: Either of the following modifications generate the correct code:Option 1:For all input signal dimensions that are of size 1, specify the Selector block with Starting index(dialog), Index 1, and Output Size 1.  In the case of the previous example, make this modification to Input 3.Option 2:Make sure that at least one index along a dimension following the size 1 dimension selects a slice with output size greater than 1.  In the previous example, you can modify Input 4 to Starting index(dialog), Index 2, and Output Size 2, or Input vector (port) and Output Size 2.

Summary: When executed, standalone application or shared library component built on Mac OS X may not be able to find NIB file.
Description: When executed, the standalone application or shared library component built on Mac OS X may display the following error: unable to find nib named: MWOpenAccessoryView in bundle path: (null) [NSViewController loadView] could not load the "MWOpenAccessoryView" nib  This occurs if the standalone application or shared library component contains code which calls the MATLAB function uigetfile .
Workaround: Copy matlabroot/bin/maci64/StartMATLAB.app/Contents/Resources/English.lproj/MWOpenAccessoryView.nib andmatlabroot/bin/maci64/StartMATLAB.app/Contents/Resources/English.lproj/MWSaveAccessoryView.nib toContents/Resources/English.lproj in your application bundle.Your application bundle is the directory structure your_application_name.app in your build directory.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Failure to detect loop involving Action subsystem and Merge block leads to incorrect simulation and code generation
Description: In the R2011a release, Simulink may fail to detect a loop that results in a data dependency violation for either an If Action Subsystem or a Switch Case Action Subsystem. This issue arises only  when: The output of such a subsystem is connected to a Merge block.  The Merge block output is connected back to an input of the same subsystem.    The attached model illustrates this issue. When a model has the problem described above, it produces incorrect simulation results, instead of producing an error. Additionally, the code generated from such a model may produce results that differ from the simulation results. To check whether this issue affects your model in the R2011a release, run the script DetectForBugReport728294.p  attached. See the Workaround  section below for details.
Workaround: Download the attached script DetectForBugReport728294.p to a folder and add the folder to the MATLAB path using the command addpath. Run the script on your model using the following syntax:ret = DetectForBugReport728294('modelname')For a model that is unaffected by this issue, the script returns an empty array. For an affected model, the script returns an array of structures with the following two fields:ActionSubsystemInLoop: An If Action or Switch Case Action subsystem that is involved in the data dependency violation described in the Description section above.MergeBlocksInLoop: A vector of Merge blocks involved in the data dependency violation described in the Description section above.If the model is affected by this issue, rewire the connections to resolve the loop that caused the data dependency violation.

To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell:unzip -l <POLYSPACE>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <POLYSPACE> folder.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell: cd <POLYSPACE>unzip <.zip file>Check that binaries have appropriate ¡®read & execute¡¯ permissions.Delete the .zip file in the <POLYSPACE> folder.

Summary: Target Language Compiler might garble multi-byte character in comments
Description: The Target Language Compiler (TLC) does not have native support for processing multi-byte characters. If a comment contains multi-byte characters, where the last byte of a multi-byte character is '\' (0x5c)  and the multi-byte character is immediately followed by a control character such as newline , then TLC might interpret the '\'  and the control character as an escape sequence. In this case, TLC might garble both characters.
Workaround: Insert a non-control character such as '.' directly after the multi-byte character ending with '\' (0x5c).

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Uncompilable code generated for a model with variant subsystems
Description: Simulink might generate uncompilable code for a model with a Variant Subsystem block under the following conditions: On the Variant Subsystem block parameter dialog, the Generate preprocessor conditionals  parameter is selected. On the Optimization  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the parameter Maximum stack size (bytes)  is set to a low value.  In this case, the generated code uses undeclared variables and does not compile.
Workaround: On the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, increase the value of the Maximum stack size (bytes) parameter.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Interpolation Using Prelookup block might produce different results for simulation and code generation when fraction port data type is floating point and intermediate results data type is fixed point with messy slope and bias
Description: The Interpolation Using Prelookup block might produce different results for simulation and code generation when the following conditions are satisfied: The fraction port data type is floating point. The table data type is floating point. The intermediate results data type is fixed point, with messy slope and bias. The product of the table data and fraction data falls outside the range of the intermediate data type. Saturation on integer overflow is off.
Workaround: The simplest workaround is to set the intermediate results data type to be the same as the table data type.

Summary: Unit Delay or Zero-Order Hold block with problematic sample time setting might produce inefficient code or incorrect results
Description: Before Simulink software introduced the Rate Transition block for handling transitions between different sample rates in a model, a Unit Delay or Zero-Order Hold block could be used to handle rate transitions, provided that the sample time for the block was correctly set. For backward compatibility, this use of a Unit Delay or Zero-Order Hold block is still permitted. However, a Unit Delay or Zero-Order Hold block might generate incorrect numerical results if both of the following conditions are true: The sample time of the Unit Delay or Zero-Order Hold block differs from both its source block sample time and its destination block sample time. The model option Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer  is selected.  Under these conditions, incorrect automatic insertion of Rate Transition blocks might occur, and both simulation and code generation might produce incorrect numerical results.
Workaround: You should avoid the modeling pattern in which the sample time of a Unit Delay or Zero-Order Hold block differs from both its source block sample time and its destination block sample time. This modeling pattern produces inefficient code.If this modeling pattern is unavoidable, to work around this bug, use Rate Transition blocks to prevent rate transitions at both the inport and outport of the Unit Delay or Zero-Order Hold block. The attached script can help you detect a problematic Unit Delay or Zero-Order Hold block. Then you can manually insert Rate Transition blocks at both the inport and outport of the block.

Summary: Signals with custom storage class ImportFromFile are initialized in generated code
Description: In version R2011a of Embedded Coder, if you specify the custom storage class ImportFromFile  for a signal with an empty InitialValue  or for a root inport/outport signal, initalization code for that signal with an Embedded Real-Time target is included in the generated code. This behavior is incorrect, and the correct behavior is for Embedded Coder to omit the initialization code for such signals.
Workaround: Use one of the following approaches.Upgrade to version R2011b of Embedded CoderUse the built-in storage class ImportedExtern instead of ImportFromFile for the signal. Note that the ImportedExtern class does not contain the CustomAttributes property.For an internal signal, explicity set the InitialValue to a non-empty value such as 0.0

Summary: Referenced models with subsystem code variants and libraries may not rebuild properly when Rebuild is set to "If any changes in known dependencies"
Description: A referenced model will not automatically be rebuilt if all  of these conditions exist: Configuration Parameters > Model Referencing > Rebuild  for the top model is set to If any changes in known dependencies . The referenced model uses subsystem code variants. The only changes to that referenced model occur in libraries that are in inactive subsystem variants.
Workaround: Set Configuration Parameters > Model Referencing > Rebuild to If any changes detected.

Summary: False red OOP check, in class analyzer mode, on abstract classes with pure virtual methods
Description: In class analyzer mode, Polyspace allows you to verify a pure virtual class, and gives checks for methods that will be called later for subclasses of the virtual class. In class analyzer mode, virtual methods can be called. But for a pure virtual class, calling a virtual method will lead to a red check. Example: class A{public: A() {} ; virtual void f() = 0; virtual void g() { } ; void do_it(A* a) { a->f() ; a->g() ; // wrong red OOP check int *p = reinterpret_cast<int*>(a); // mandatory to have the OOP red in this example because it prevents some optimisations that will remove this red check }};

Summary: xpcsetCC('setup') function cannot locate installed Microsoft Visual C++ 2010 Express compiler.
Description: xpcsetCC('setup') function cannot locate installed Microsoft Visual C++ 2010 Express compiler.
Workaround: Call xpcsetCC(type, location) function to manually set compiler. For example:xpcsetCC('VisualC','C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio 10.0')????

Summary: Links from verification results to Simulink model do not work
Description: For source code generated with Embedded Coder software, when you launch verification from outside of Simulink, the links in the source code that trace run-time checks back to the model do not work. Enabling back to model links in the Polyspace Verification Environment (using the option Review > Code Generator Support > Embedded Coder ) does not fix this problem.
Workaround: To workaround this issue:In the results folder, open the file: code_generator_used.Replace 'Embedded Coder' with 'RTWEmbeddedCoder'.Save the file.Reload the results in the Polyspace Verification Environment.

Summary: Generated code can fail to compile when using variants with preprocessor conditionals
Description: The code generated for a model that uses variants with preprocessor conditionals can fail to compile if the variant references data of a custom storage class whose variable is defined in the code using the static keyword. The problem is caused by the static keyword preceding the preprocessor conditional, e.g. static #if UseSS2  real_T InBetSig = 0.0;#endif            when instead it should succeed the preprocessor conditional, e.g. #if UseSS2  static real_T InBetSig = 0.0;#endif            One example of such a storage class is 'FileScope', which may be used by mpt.Signal and mpt.Parameter objects.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Change log: May 12, 2011: attached attachment_726567_11a_2011-05-12.zip for 2011a only

Summary: Generating code for a referenced model may fail to compile if a model with no Model blocks has previously generated code in the same directory
Description: The following steps result in generated code that does not compile: Start in a directory that does not contain an slprj  directory,\. Generate code for a model that does not have Model blocks, and has the parameter  on the Configuration Parameters  > Code Generation  > Interface  >  Utility code generation  parameter set to Shared location . Generate code for a model that contains a Model block.  These steps result in a compiler error when the generated code is compiled. The compiler error varies by platform, but is similar to the example shown below: sldemo_mdlref_counter.c: In function 'mr_sldemo_mdlref_counter':sldemo_mdlref_counter.c:77: error: 'sldemo_mdlref_counter_TimingBridge' undeclared (first use in this function)sldemo_mdlref_counter.c:77: error: (Each undeclared identifier is reported only oncesldemo_mdlref_counter.c:77: error: for each function it appears in.)
Workaround: Delete the following file in the slprj directory:slprj/ert/_sharedutils/rtwtypes.hDeleting that file causes Simulink to regenerate the file with the necessary information. If you are using a system target file other than ert.tlc, replace the directory name ert with your aystem target file name.

Summary: Loading initial state may cause an error if the model contains a From Workspace block
Description: If you simulate a model and save the complete SimState in the final state, then loading the final SimState as initial state to the same model may cause the following error at the start of simulation: ??? Simulink cannot load the initial SimState because the model,(modelname), was changed after the SimState was saved. Run the simulation again and resave the SimState. This issue may appear rarely and only when the From Workspace meets both of these conditions: The Sample time  parameter is set to continuous (0 ). The Enable zero-crossing detection  parameter is enabled.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink generates incorrect code when a For Each subsystem degenerates to have a single iteration and an output that is concatenated along a singleton dimension
Description: Simulink generates incorrect code for a For Each subsystem that satisfies all  of the following conditions: The For Each subsystem does not generate a code reuse function. The number of iterations is 1. An example where such a situation occurs is when a [2x1] input signal of a For Each subsystem is configured to be partitioned along dimension 1 with partition width of 2. A For Each subsystem output is configured to be concatenated along a dimension that has a size of 1 within one iteration. The source of this output is a global variable in the generated code.  To check whether this issue affects your model in R2010a, R2010b, or R2011a releases, run the attached DetectForBugReport725981.p  script. See the "Workaround" section below for details.
Workaround: Download the attached DetectForBugReport725981.p script to a folder.Use the addpath command to add the folder to the MATLAB path.Run the script on your model using the following syntax:      ret = DetectForBugReport725981('modelname') For a model that is unaffected by this issue, the script returns an empty array.For an affected model, the script returns an array of structures, with the following two fields:ForEachSS: The For Each subsystem that meets the three conditions described in the "Bug Description" section above.ForEachSSOutputs: The indices of the outputs mentioned in the condition above.Use one of the following two workarounds for the For Each subsystems in your affected model:For all For Each subsystems identified in ForEachSS, set the Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Real-Time Workshop system code parameter to Code Reuse.Convert every For Each subsystem identified in ForEachSS into a non-iterative subsystem by removing the For Each block.

Summary: Unexpected eliminated block comment in model.c file
Description: An eliminated block comment might appear in an incorrect location in the model.c  file. This comment has the following style: /* S-Function Block: 'Block1'  * * Regarding 'Block1': *  Unused code path elimination  */
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation (formerly Real Time Workshop) > Comments pane, clear the Show eliminated blocks parameter. You can find information about eliminated blocks in the Traceability Report section of the Code Generation Report.

Summary: Error when exporting multiple AUTOSAR runnables
Description: When you export multiple AUTOSAR runnables from a wrapper subsystem and an Outport  block of a function-call subsystem specifies that its initial condition comes from its input signal, the software error may produce an error similar to the following:  Unexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR: rtwcg_utils/rtwcg_autosar_transform.cpp line 139: isOutputFcn".  Please report this to MathWorks if you can cause it to recur### Starting build procedure for model: Function0### Generating code into build folder: /local/mkachmar/tmp/tpc71d5724_uk-mkachmar0l/Function0_autosar_rtwCGIR assertion 'isOutputFcn' failed in 'rtwcg_utils/rtwcg_autosar_transform.cpp:139'

Summary: Warning "Undefined function or variable 'HDLSynthFilePostfix" appears when generating synthesis script for ISE
Description: When you select Xilinx ISE  as the synthesis tool in the HDL Code Generation > EDA Tool Scripts  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box and generate code, you get the message: ??? Undefined function or variable 'HDLSynthFilePostfix'.
Workaround: To work around this issue, follow these steps:Select another tool from the drop-down menu (for example, Mentor Graphics Precision) even if you want to generate a synthesis script for the ISE.Copy the Synthesis initialization, Synthesis command, and Synthesis termination scripts from the ISE option to this tool option.

Summary: Polyspace verification stops in 'Software Safety Analysis Level 0' phase, with the message: "(P_SULV) @FFT: expected struct ty !"
Description: Lauching a verification on C++ code, Polyspace verification stops in 'Software Safety Analysis Level 0' phase, with the following error message: **** Software Safety Analysis Level 0 - 9 (P_DAL) **** Software Safety Analysis Level 0 - 9 (P_DAL) took 16.6real, 15.7u + 0.9s (0.4gc) **** Software Safety Analysis Level 0 - 10 (P_SULV) ---------------------------------------------------------------------- @FFT: expected struct ty ! |An internal consistency problem has been detected.   The reason for the error is code-specific. If you experience this error and if workaround specified below does not work, send the affected code to customer support. We will investigate the problem and provide another workaround, if possible.
Workaround: Relaunch your verification using the option -D const=

Summary: GUI layout incorrect on Mac? platform
Description: MATLAB? versions prior to R2011a for the Mac platform may incorrectly report the value for the screensize  property. The incorrect value may vary by MATLAB release and Mac OS X version, starting in MATLAB R2007a and the Mac OS X 10.4.7 Software Update, respectively. Depending on the screensize  value may result in incorrect GUI lay out on the Mac platform.

Summary: Error when generating SIL block for model containing global data stores
Description: It is possible for an error to occur while generating a SIL block for a model containing global data stores, which are of a non-addressable storage class. For example, the BitField  storage class, which can be specified for Simulink.Signal  and mpt.Signal  objects, is non-addressable. The TLC interpreter reports the error, similar to the following: Error: File: /local/mkachmar/work/aslrtw2/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/lib/utillib.tlc Line: 642 Column: 8%exit directive: Simulink Coder Fatal: Can not call custom storage class version 1 with LibAccessCustomData unless the fifth argument is empty

Summary: MATLAB Builder EX Function Wizard doesn't work on machines with Japanese language OS (2011a)
Description: When you start the Function Wizard, selecting the workflow labeled I want to create, debug, build and package MATLAB functions using MATLAB Builder EX , if you choose to create a new Deployment Tool project, a message box displays the following error message:Failed to set the class name for the Deploytool project .... The project currently opened in Deploytool is not for Excel Add-in target.  If you choose to open an existing Deployment Tool project, the error message is:Failed to get class name for project .... The project currently opened in Deploytool is not for Excel Add-in target.
Workaround: Install the files in the attachment as follows:Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.Locate your MATLAB installation by entering the command matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. The return value of matlabroot is the location of your MATLAB installation.Close MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file and save it in matlabroot.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. Open the .zip file to view its contents.Extract the files from the .zip file to matlabroot.Delete the .zip file in matlabroot.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation fails if CAN Receive Blocks f() Output Port is not connected to a Function-Call Subsystem
Description: CAN Receive  block will generate the following error during code generation if you do not connect the CAN Receive  block output port, f() , to a function-call subsystem that contains the Unpack  block: Code generation assertion 'eIdx >= 0 && eIdx < numFunctionCallDests(pIdx)' failed in '.\sl_engin\rtwcg_block_impl.cpp:2275'[ 0] 0x1c545207 C:/Program Files/MATLAB/R2011a/bin/win32/rtwcg.dll+00283143 ( RTWCG::RTWCGCtx::callFunctionCallDest+000455 )[ 1] 0x2111d8ff C:/Program Files/MATLAB/R2011a/toolbox/vnt/vntblks/vntmex/svntcanreceive.mexw32+00055551 ( mexFunction+017695 )
Workaround: You must use a Function-Call Subsystem to unpack CAN messages during code generation. If your model requires unpacking in this case without a Function-Call Subsystem, contact MathWorks technical support.

Summary: Models with a configured model function prototype and a root-level trigger port fail if the trigger port name changes
Description: A model that meets both of the following conditions fails if you change the name of the trigger port: Has a trigger port at the root level, with the Trigger type  set to either rising , falling , or either  Configures a model function prototype for either C or C++ code generation  The failure prevents you from opening the dialog to configure the model interface, and causes a segmentation violation when you generate code.

Summary: Incorrect date sorting in the Polyspace Queue Manager Interface (Spooler)
Description: The Date column in the Polyspace Queue Manager Interface (Spooler) is sorted incorrectly. The Date column is sorted alphabetically, instead of chronologically. ?
Workaround: Sort verifications by ID.This will sort verifcations in the order they were submitted.

Summary: #undef in the generated code for model with a single-instance referenced model
Description: The generated code for a model that meets both  of these conditions contains a #undef: The model contains a Model block. The Model block references a model that has the Configuration Parameters > Model Referencing > Total number of instances allowed per top model  parameter set to One .   For example, the generated code may be similiar to this: /* Child system includes */ #define sub_MDLREF_HIDE_CHILD_ #include "sub.h"#undef sub_MDLREF_HIDE_CHILD_  The use of #undef macros violates MISRA-C rule 19.6. ?

Summary: Error message indicates the wrong port when a data type mismatch occurs in the HDL Cosimulation block.
Description: In the HDL Cosimulation block, EDA Simulator Link reports an error when the bit width of the Simulink signal connected to one of the ports does not match the bit width of the corresponding HDL signal. Due to the existence of this bug, the error message shows the wrong port that has the data type mismatch. For example, suppose you have one input port named "/top/in1" and one output port named "/top/out1" in the design, and the bit width of the Simulink signal connected to port "/top/in1" does not match the bit width of the corresponding HDL signal. When updating or starting the cosimulation model, EDA Simulator Link displays the following error message:"Error reported by S-function 'shdlcosim' in 'mymdl/Subsystem/S-Function': Data type mismatch at signal '/top/out1'.This port expects a Logic data type of size 1"In this error message, the wrong port - "/top/out1" - is indicated to have the data type mismatch. The correct error message should show port "/top/in1" and the expected bit width.
Workaround: When a data type mismatch error occurs during model update (similar to the example in Bug Description), check all input and output ports for the potential data type mismatch rather than looking at only the port indicated in the error message.

Summary: atan2 function in the Trigonometric Function block does not produce an error for complex inputs
Description: The atan2 function in the Trigonometric Function block does not support complex inputs. However, the block does not produce a warning or an error for this condition.  Instead, the block silently uses the real parts of both inputs to produce the result in simulation.

Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <POLYSPACE> folder.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell: cd <POLYSPACE>unzip <.zip file>Check that binaries have appropriate ¡®read & execute¡¯ permissions.Delete the .zip files in the <POLYSPACE> folderNote that patch has to be installed on the client side.?

Summary: Structured text code generated for 2-D Lookup Table blocks do not work with Rockwell Automation RSLogix 5000 IDEs
Description: Generated structured text for an atomic subsystem with a 2-D Lookup Table block contains nested indexing operations (such as, fcn150_bp01Data[fcn150_maxIndex[0]] ). This structured text code cannot be used on the Rockwell Automation RSLogix 5000 IDE as this IDE does not support  nested indexing operations.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible wrong value of a for-loop index variable after last iteration.
Description: When generating code from a MATLAB algorithm that contains a for -loop, the index variable might be assigned a wrong value after the last loop iteration if all  of the following conditions are met: The for -loop is located inside an if  or switch  statement. The index variable is assigned outside  the loop from a constant value. The assignment occurs outside the if or switch statement, or in a different branch of it. The index variable of the for -loop is used  outside  of the loop.  This bug does not affect the for -loop, but subsequent use of the index variable might propagate an incorrect value. This bug occurs: In MATLAB Function blocks (formerly Embedded MATLAB Function blocks). When generating code from MATLAB using codegen  (formerly emlmex  and emlc ) and fiaccel  commands.
Workaround: Refactor the code so that the for-loop index variable is not used outside the loop.

Summary: FIL speed grade settings fails (2011A Release)
Description: The FPGA-in-the-Loop application sets the speed grade of each FPGA on the supported board using the UCF file. In release 2011a, the FIL speed grade settings are not being applied properly during project generation. If you are using ISE version 12 or earlier, the Xilinx project uses a default setting and ignores the FIL setting. You do not get any warning. If you are using ISE version 13, the FIL setting is applied, but the bitstream is not generated, and you get an error. The attached patch makes it so the FIL settings are applied and the bitstream is generated successfully for release 2011A and any supported version of ISE. This problem is fixed in the 2011B release for all supported ISE versions.

Summary: Tasks stay in pending state when running multiple jobs with generic scheduler in a nonshared file system
Description: If you are using the generic scheduler with a nonshared filesystem, your first job might run successfully, but subsequent jobs might reach the finished  state while all tasks stay in the pending  state with no error messages. If you are running matlabpool interactively, opening the first matlabpool session might succeed, but subsequent matlabpools might fail with the following errror: ??? Error using ==> distcomp.abstractscheduler.matlabpool at 74The interactive parallel job finished without any messages. If you look in the log files in the job's DataLocation , you see the following message: Error using ==> distcomp.filestorage.pGetConstructorsFromMetadata at 23The storage metadata file does not exist or is corruptError in ==> distcomp.filestorage.getProxyByName at 22    constructor = storage.pGetConstructorsFromMetadata(parent, ID);Error in ==> distcomp_evaluate_filetask>iSetup at 365    [jobProxy, jobConstructor] = storage.getProxyByName(runprop.JobLocation);Error in ==> distcomp_evaluate_filetask at 19    runprop = iSetup(handlers, varargin); Note that the exact line numbers listed in the error message might be different, depending on the release of MATLAB that you are using. For distributed jobs, the logs are written to dataLocation /JobN/TaskM.log , where N  is the job ID and M  is the task ID. For parallel jobs, the logs files are written to dataLocation /JobN/JobN.mpiexec.log , where N  is the job ID. To be able to view the logs for an interactive matlabpool session, run this command in MATLAB before launching the matlabpool: >>pctconfig('preservejobs', true) The matlabpool logs are written to dataLocation /JobN/JobN.mpiexec.log , where N  is the job ID. You can find the job ID of the interactive matlabpool by looking at the ID of the last job that is listed in the scheduler's Jobs  property immediately after you have tried to open matlabpool. After you have viewed the logs, be sure to destroy the job associated with the matlabpool session, so that the job's files are removed from the datalocation. Before running matlabpool again from the same MATLAB client session using any other scheduler (such as local), set the preservejobs  option back to false , so that the job files associated with subsequent matlabpool sessions are destroyed. >>pctconfig('preservejobs', false) This bug pertains only to using the generic scheduler example scripts for nonshared file systems that are shipped in matlabroot /toolbox/distcomp/examples/integration , or if you have written your own generic scheduler integration that uses the parallel.cluster.RemoteClusterAccess  class.

Description: Building an xPC Target application causes MATLAB to crash if: The build is configured with the option "Build COM objects from tagged signals/parameters", and The instrumentation model is generated using the "xpcsliface" command.  ? ?

This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Close MATLAB.Download MATLAB file "xpctagbio.m" attached to this report and copy it over the original version here:<MATLAB>\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc\privateLaunch MATLABType the following command at the MATLAB command prompt:rehash toolbox???

Summary: Paths to files specified using the -include option are lost when you move your Project folder.
Description: Source files and include folders specified in the Project Manager are considered to have relative paths to the project folder, if they are located within a subfolder of the project folder. Therefore, if you move the project folder and its subfolders (with the source files and include directories om them), the paths to the source files and include folders are automatically updated to the new location when you open the project. However, files specified using the -include  option are not considered to have relative paths, so those files can be lost when you move your project folder.

Summary: Mux signals logged in Simulink.TsArray format have incorrect dimension when inside of Accelerator mode Model blocks
Description: When logging a mux vector using Simulink signal logging, incorrect sample dimension are logged if all of the following conditions exist: The signal is inside of a reference model. The Model block is in Accelerator mode. Configuration Parameters > Data Import/Export > Signal logging format  parameter is set to ModelDataLogs .
Workaround: In R2011a or later releases, set the Signal logging format parameter to Dataset.In earlier releases, set the Model block to Normal mode.

Summary: On a Linux workstation, changing the port used to communicate with the Polyspace Metrics web interface has no effect
Description: On a Linux workstation, changing the port used to communicate with the Polyspace Metrics web interface in the Polyspace Preferences 'Server configuration' tab does not work. The port used is still the default value.
Workaround: To workaround this issue:In the Polyspace Verification Environment, select Options > Preferences > Server configuration.In the 'Port used to communicate with the Polyspace Metrics web interface' field, change the port number.Click OK to save the new setting and close the Polyspace UI.Copy the file: $HOME/.polyspace/polyspace.prf to the folder: $HOME/polyspace

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Rate Transition block gives incorrect simulation results when used with a variable-size signal as input in NoOp, Copy, and ZOH modes
Description: The simulation and build of a model that contains a Rate Transition block with both of the following characteristics results in incorrect simulation and generated code: Input is a variable-size signal Simulation behavior mode is NoOp, Copy, or ZOH  The simulation results may contain uninitialized values. The simulation and code generation may also result in incorrect run-time signal dimensions.

Summary: While reviewing results in the Polyspace Run-Time Checks perspective, the software displays results from another user
Description: While reviewing your results in the Polyspace User Interface, you suddenly see results from another user. This happens when another user logs in to the same physical machine at the same time, and downloads his results using Polyspace Spooler or a batch command. This error happens when each login account uses same port number associated to Polyspace Metrics. You can see the port number by selecting Options > Preferences > Server Configuration  in the Polyspace Verifier Environment (PVE). The port number is displayed under 'Port used to communicate with the Polyspace Metrics web interface'. ?
Workaround: Change the port number used to communicate with Polyspace Metrics. If it concerns Linux accounts, please see also Bug Report 721671.

Summary: HDL code generation time for some large Simulink models might be unexpectedly long
Description: For some large Simulink models with deep subsystem hierarchies (more than 1000 subsystems and more than 10,000 total blocks), HDL code generation time might take several (10+) hours. To confirm that your model has this issue, follow these steps: Partition your (top) DUT-level subsystem into 3 or 4 smaller subsystems. Run HDL code generation for each of the smaller subsystems individually. (You will have to manually move these subsystems to the root level of the model). Run HDL code generation for the original model (without partitioning).  The run time in step 3 should be approximately equal to the sum of the run times in step 2. If the step 3 run time is much larger than the sum of the run times in step 2, your model might be affected by this bug. Please contact us so we can confirm that this is the case.

Summary: Property "HDLSynthTool" was misdocumented as "SynthToolOption" in the documentation
Description: Property "HDLSynthTool" was misdocumented as "SynthToolOption" in the R2011a documentation. If you try to use SynthToolOption, you will get an error.
Workaround: Use "HDLSynthTool" to select synthesis tool for which the coder generates scripts.

Summary: When selecting the parameter "Allow tasks to execute concurrently", the real-time execution may use different initial condition values than were used during the simulation in Simulink
Description: When the xPC Target parameter "Allow tasks to execute concurrently " is selected, there can be a difference between simulation and real-time execution behavior in the use of the Initial Condition value of the Rate Transition block. When the Rate Transition block parameter "Ensure data integrity during data transfer"  is selected, Simulink simulates the execution behavior based on the following assumptions: Rate monotonic scheduler is used (faster periodic tasks run before slower periodic tasks when both have a sample time) Task execution time is set to 1/¡Þ (zero time to execute a task)  If the Rate Transition block inport sample time is faster than the outport sample time (a fast to slow transition) , the Initial Condition specified at the Rate Transition block will not be used during simulation because the faster task will always update (overwrite) it before the slower task can read it. However, during real-time execution on a multi-core target system, the slower task may run concurrently with the faster task and read the initial condition value of the Rate Transition block before the faster task can update it. Both scenarios are valid and possible in the real-time execution environment. Because the Initial Condition value of the Rate Transition block is zero by default and the algorithmic code contained in the slower task may not be intended to process a zero value, using the Initial Condition value can lead to a divide-by-zero error or some other numerical exception during real-time execution which does not occur during simulation. Because this 'worst-case' numerical behavior occurs when the Initial Condition value is read, Simulink should simulate that scenario.
Workaround: To simulate an Initial Condition value for the Rate Transition block, insert a separate Initial Condition (IC) block at the outport of any Rate Transition block being used in a fast to slow periodic rate transition. Specify the desired initial condition value on the IC block.

Summary: Acquisitions using the GigE Vision adaptor may fail due to lost packets
Description: Acquisitions using the GigE Vision adaptor may fail due to lost packets. One way to help improve performance is to set the packet delay appropriately. The R2011a release of the toolbox sets the packet delay to 0 during the start of acquisition, overwriting any previous value you may have configured using other software. This patch allows you to set the packet delay in your camera via your vendor's software or through a tool such as JAI's Camera Control Tool, and then have the packet delay not be reset by the GigE Vision adaptor.

Summary: Calling save_system on a partially-loaded library deletes the unloaded part of the library
Description: When Simulink encounters a library link in a model, it loads only the required block from the library. The remainder of the library is loaded only if its window is made visible. If you call save_system on a partially-loaded library, only the loaded portion of the library is saved, overwriting the file and deleting any unloaded parts. You can only encounter this bug at the command line with save_system. You can save correctly using the Simulink Editor.
Workaround: Avoid calling save_system on a library that is not fully loaded.  Partially-loaded libraries will rarely have unsaved changes, so saving them is not usually necessary.  For safety, if there's any chance that the library has not already been fully loaded, call load_system before save_system, e.g.load_system(libname);save_system(libname);Alternatively you can avoid this problem by saving using the Simulink Editor.

Summary: SegV when simulating a reference model that contains global data stores
Description: Simulating a top model that references models that access global data stores can cause a segmentation violation when both these conditions are met. The reference models are independent of sample time The parameter Periodic sample time constraint  for the reference models is not set to Ensure sample time independent
Workaround: If a reference model is sample-time independent, set its parameter Periodic sample time constraint to Ensure sample time independent.Note: To determine if a reference model is sample-time independent, set its parameter Periodic sample time constraint to Ensure sample time independent and then perform an update diagram. If the model is not sample-time independent, Simulink will generate an error.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect index in logging code for grt_malloc and multi-instance ERT
Description: When Simulink Coder (formerly Real-Time Workshop) generates code for grt_malloc  or multi-instance ERT with MAT-file logging enabled, the logging code uses the incorrect index if any of the output signals cannot be logged. For example, buses and enumerated data type signals cannot be logged. The incorrect index can cause invalid pointer access and an exception when the generated code is run. During code generation, Simulink Coder (formerly Real-Time Workshop) generates the following warning to inform you when ouput signal data cannot be logged: Warning: In block "...", block type "Outport": Unable to log signal driving this block because MAT-File logging is not supported for data type '...'.
Workaround: Use one of the following approaches:Disable MAT-file logging in the generated codeSelect a system target file other than grt_malloc.tlcDisable multi-instance code generation for ERT

Summary: S-Function does not compile for model with Stateflow machine-parented data
Description: The autogenerated S-function fails to build for a model, when the model contains Stateflow charts with machine-parented data.
Workaround: Try replacing the machine-parented data with Data Store Memory blocks. For more information, enter this web command at the MATLAB prompt:web([docroot '/toolbox/stateflow/ug/bqfj9xz.html'])

Summary: Invalid links from Freescale MPC5xx blocks to product help
Description: With some MPC5xx blocks, if you open the block and click the Help  button, or if you right-click the block and select the "Help for..." submenu, the software displays an error message: Invalid map file: C:\Program Files (x86)\MATLAB\R2011a\help\en\toolbox\mpc555dk\mpc555dk.map Specified map file does not exist: Or Specified topic (<topic id>) does not exist in the map file. <path to the map file>\ecoder\helptargets.map. To reproduce this error message, open any demo that contains MPC5xx blocks, such as mpc555rt_led : Open the subsystem, Target_Led.  Right-click the MIOS Digital out  block and select Help . See the error dialog stating specified topic id does not exist.

Summary: gammaincinv may incorrectly warn and return NaN
Description: The gammaincinv  function may incorrectly generate a warning and return NaN  when called with very small values for the first input and very large values for the second input (gamma shape parameter). The following code demonstrates this issue: >> gammaincinv(1e-16,2e307,'upper')Warning: Inverse upper incomplete gamma did not converge for p = 1e-016, a = 2e+307. ans =   NaN

Summary: Model block arguments are not evaluated correctly when the Model block is under a mask in a referenced model
Description: The model argument value of a Model block may not get evaluated correctly if all of the following are true: The Inline parameters  setting of the top model is set to On. There is a Model block under a mask that references a model that contains model arguments. This Model block lives in a referenced model that is referenced in Normal mode. One of the Model argument values in this Model block references a parameter from that mask.  In this case, Simulink does not push down the Model argument value from the Model block to the referenced model.

Summary: Simulink Accelerator fails to regenerate code when it should for one situation
Description: For the following situation, the Simulik Accelerator mode fails to regenerate code when it should: A parameter value is changed on a block mask at the root level of the model (for example, not inside a subsystem). The only use of the parameter under the block mask is inside a non-virtual subsystem. This subsystem might be the masked block itself, or it might be a non-virtual subsystem contained inside the masked subsystem.  These conditions cause the old value of the parameter to be used for the Accelerator mode simulation. The new parameter value should be used.

Summary: "Hide MATLAB" doesn't work properly on Mac
Description: In MATLAB R2011a for Mac, MATLAB does not stay hidden when the user selects "MATLAB > Hide MATLAB" (or the keyboard shortcut equivalent, Cmd-H). MATLAB's windows disappear briefly but reappear in the background.
Workaround: This bug is a side effect of moving MATLAB menus to the top of the screen in R2011a (see the Update on MATLAB for the Mac). The bug will be fixed in a future version of MATLAB; until then, the only workaround is to disable the top-of-the-screen menubar.To disable the Mac screen menubar, you can modify the java.opts file by adding the following line:-Dapple.laf.useScreenMenuBar=falseThat line instructs Java to disable the Mac screen menubar.A java.opts file can reside in the folder from which you launch MATLAB or in the bin/maci64 subfolder within the MATLAB root folder. A java.opts file in the latter location applies to all users, but individual users might not have permissions to modify a java.opts file there. If there is a java.opts file in both locations with settings that conflict, the setting in the java.opts file in the folder from which you launch MATLAB takes precedence. You might want to check both locations to see whether you have existing java.opts files and then decide which one to modify.To create a new java.opts file or modify an existing copy in the folder from which you launch MATLAB:Quit MATLAB.Relaunch MATLAB and immediately enter the following line in the Command Window:edit java.optsTo create or modify a java.opts file that applies to all users, you can enter the following line in the MATLAB Command Window at any time:edit(fullfile(matlabroot,'bin','maci64','java.opts'))

Summary: If a Simulink model contains a callback function that uses Handle Graphics, the Signal Builder block interface might be corrupted
Description: If you simulate a Simulink model and the following is true: Model contains callback function that uses a Handle Graphics plot object The Signal Builder block user interface is open at simulation  The Signal Builder block user interface might be corrupted.
Workaround: As a workaround, modify the Simulink model callback function to be robust by using a designated figure window.For example, change the model callback function fromplot(rand(1:5));toh=figure;set(0,'CurrentFigure',h);plot(rand(1:5));

Summary: Documentation for the generalized extreme value distribution pdf, cdf, icdf, and random functions shows the ¦Ì and ¦Ò parameters in the wrong order.
Description: The documentation for the pdf , cdf , icdf , and random  functions includes a table describing the distribution parameters and their order in the argument list for each supported distribution. For the generalized extreme value distribution, the scale parameter ¦Ò and the location parameter ¦Ì are shown in the incorrect order.  The documentation for the gevpdf , gevcdf , gevinv , gevrnd , and gevstat  functions is correct.
Workaround: The correct order of the parameters for the generalized extreme value distribution is (k,¦Ò,¦Ì), where k is the shape parameter, ¦Ò is the scale parameter, and ¦Ì is the location parameter. For example, the following command generates a 2-by-2 array of random values from the generalized extreme value distribution with shape k=1, scale ¦Ò=.5, and location ¦Ì=-.5:k = 1; sigma = .5; mu = -.5;random('gev',k,sigma,mu,[2 2])ans =     1.4401   -0.7577    4.0533    4.5183

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code for a triggered Function-Call Subsystem block is not calling the terminate function
Description: The generated code for a Function-Call Subsystem block is not invoking the terminate function, except when the block is triggered by the Function-Call Generator block from the Simulink library. This issue exists in R2011a and R2010bSP1.

Summary: To Workspace logged variables may appear incorrect in a code reuse subsystem in Rapid Accelerator mode
Description: When you use Rapid Accelerator mode to simulate a model that has a To Workspace block inside a code reuse subsystem that is used multiple times in single model, then only 1 variable may be logged. When simulating in Normal mode, each To Workspace block instance logs a separate variable.
Workaround: Use one of the following approaches:Disable code reuse for the subsystem. In future Simulink releases, subsystems with To Workspace blocks will not reuse code. Simulate the model in Normal mode.

Summary: blp.timeseries reports the wrong timestamps for data in a different daylight saving time zone
Description: A user requests data after the daylight saving time changeover for a date that falls before the time change. The timestamps are reported to be one hour earlier than they should be.

Summary: Polyspace verification stops in normalization phase, with the message: "@FFT: Expression on address of empty routine"
Description: Lauching a verification on C++ code using the main generator, Polyspace stops in source normalization phase, with the following error message: **** C++ source normalization - 2 (P_INIT)  * Generating the Main ...----------------------------------------------------------------------<pathto>\myfile.cpp, line XX (column YY): @FFT: Expression on address of empty routine|An internal consistency problem has been detected.  At line XX  and column YY  in myfile.cpp  file, main()  program is called by another routine. This is not handled by C++ main generator.
Workaround: Relaunch your verification using the option -D main=extern_main, it will rename the function main()to external_main().Since the body of the external_main() function does not exist, Polyspace will stub it automatically.

Summary: Property "HDLSynthTool" was misdocumented as "SynthToolOption" in the documentation
Description: Property "HDLSynthTool" was misdocumented as "SynthToolOption" in the R2011a documentation. If you try to use SynthToolOption, you will get an error.
Workaround: Use "HDLSynthTool" to select synthesis tool for which the coder generates scripts.

Summary: Incorrect generated code from referenced models with reusable subsystems containing Simscape blocks
Description: Generating code for the main model when a referenced model contains a reusable subsystem with Simscape blocks could result in a compilation error, a runtime error, or a silent incorrect simulation.
Workaround: For the atomic subsystem containing Simscape blocks, change the packaging specified on the Subsystem dialog box.Right-click the subsystem and select Subsystem Parameters. The Subsystem dialog box opens.Select the Code Generation tab.Ensure that the Function Packaging drop-down list is set to Function.Click OK.

Summary: Values of minus zero may be stored as zero in V5, V6, and V7 MAT-files
Description: When saving to V5, V6, and V7 MAT-files, MATLAB compresses matrices of floating-point values that are all small integers (flints) by storing them as an integer type. Values of minus zero lose their sign bit.For information about the internal storage of MAT-files, see https://www.mathworks.com/help/pdf_doc/matlab/matfile_format.pdf.
Workaround: Workaround 1: Store the data in a V4 MAT-file or a V7.3 MAT-file.Workaround 2: Include a value in the matrix that is not an integer (such as 0.5).

Summary: gensim fails when called with sample time
Description: When you call gensim  with a sample time among its input arguments, it generates an error.
Workaround: To work around this error, if the network is static do not include a sample time in the call to gensim. For example:gensim(net) If the network is dynamic, set the sample time before calling gensim. For example: net.sampleTime = 0.1;gensim(net)

Summary: Importing data in a Signal Builder block generates errors when the block outputs a bus
Description: In Simulink 7.7 (R2011a), if you configure the Signal Builder block to send a bus of signals while importing data in one of the following ways, issues occur: Replacing existing data set, errors are displayed in the MATLAB Command Window. Using signalbuilder  function to append a new group with signal names different from the existing group of signal names, signal names in the existing group are not updated in the output bus. The signal names of the existing group and appended group should be the same in the output bus.
Workaround: Installation instructions       Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.       Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.       Exit MATLAB.       Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.                     Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file:                                    Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.                     Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>                                          Extract the files from the .zip file.                                                               Windows Systems:                                                             Open the .zip file.                                  Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.                                                                                                Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):                            cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>                                                 Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong answer for MinMax block with mix of scalar and variable-size signals
Description: If the first input to a MinMax block is a scalar and the second and subsequent inputs are variable-size signals, it is possible that the generated code produces the wrong answer. This problem is not present if All of the non-scalar inputs to the MinMax block are fixed-size. The first input to the MinMax block is non-scalar.  The attached zip file provides a model that exhibits the problem, as well as a workaround.
Workaround: Switch the input signals to a MinMax block such that the first input is not a scalar.

Summary: Generating code for multiple entry points with variable-size data might cause assertion
Description: MATLAB Coder (formerly emlc  and emlmex ) might assert when generating code for multiple entry points that use variable-size data.
Workaround: Enable dynamic memory allocation, turn off variable sizing, or generate code separately for each entry point.

Summary: Invalid analysis results when output ports of conditionally executed subsystems lack initial values
Description: You can see invalid analysis results when output ports of conditionally executed subsystems are not given initial values. The bug occurs because these output ports incorrectly remain uninitialized during analysis.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier throws error 'Inf cannot be converted to fixed-point' during Design Error Detection analysis
Description: Simulink Design Verifier throws error 'Inf cannot be converted to fixed-point ' while performing Design Error Detection analysis if your model contains fixed-point data types.

Summary: commscope.eyediagram produces an eye diagram where the last point in the Quadrature Signal of the eye diagram goes to zero
Description: commscope.eyediagram generates eye diagrams based on complex signals. The object uses the real part of the signal to generate the "In-phase Signal" eye diagram and the imaginary part of the signal to generate the "Quadrature Signal" eye diagram. The last element of the eye diagram vector is always assigned a NaN. Since the real part of a NaN is still a NaN, this point does not plot in the In-phase Signal, but since the imaginary part of a NaN is zero it is plotted in the "Quadrature Signal" as a zero.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Option -scalar-overflows-checks does not work when constant is assigned to unsigned variable
Description: Consider the following code: unsigned char x; x = 257;  If you set the option -scalar-overflows-checks  to signed-and-unsigned  and do not select the option -ignore-constant-overflows , the software should produce a red overflow check (OVFL) for the assignment x = 257 . However, the software does not produce the expected check.
Workaround: If you use the batch mode, specify one of the following options:-scalar-overflows-checks signed_and_unsigned-detect-unsigned-overflows. Note that support for this option will cease in a future release.If you use the Polyspace verification environment (PVE), update your software by following the installation instructions.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your Polyspace installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your Polyspace installation. By default this is in c:\PolySpace or in /usr/local/PolySpace on Linux systems. This folder is referred to as <POLYSPACE> in subsequent steps. PolySpace_Common and PolySpaceFor<lang>_R20xx are the two principal sub-folders of <POLYSPACE>.Exit any Polyspace Graphical User Interface that is open.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <POLYSPACE> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell:unzip -l <POLYSPACE>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <POLYSPACE> folder.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell: cd <POLYSPACE>unzip <.zip file>Check that binaries have appropriate ¡®read & execute¡¯ permissions.Delete the .zip file in the <POLYSPACE> folder.

Summary: MATLAB? shuts down when setting the ColumnFormat property for a uitable object
Description: MATLAB shuts down when you set the ColumnFormat property of a uitable object if you attempt to indicate empty cell array placeholders implicitly.  For instance, the following code causes MATLAB to shut down:t = uitable('Data',magic(3));cf = cell(1,3);cf{2} = 'numeric';set(t,'ColumnFormat',cf);
Workaround: Explicitly specify any empty cell array placeholders when setting a uitable object's ColumnFormat property.  For example:t = uitable('Data',magic(3));cf = {[] 'numeric' []};set(t,'ColumnFormat',cf);

Summary: From File block simulation time may vary slightly between Normal and Accelerator mode, causing different outputs
Description: The time value read by the From File block when in Normal mode may be slightly different numerically than when in Accelerator mode. The small difference occurs when both  of these conditions apply: The model either uses a variable step solver or uses a fixed step solver and has the tasking mode set to single tasking. The model has multiple rates.  When the From File block has the Data interpolation within time range  parameter set to: Linear interpolation , this small deviation in time causes a small deviation in the output value. Zero order hold , this small deviation in time may cause the output of a different data point in Normal mode, compared to Accelerator mode.

Summary: Error when opening the PolySpace Queue Manager Interface (Spooler)
Description: When you try to open the Polyspace Queue Manager Interface (Spooler) on a Client machine, the following error message is displayed : Error: Unkown host : <hostname>768  where hostname is the name of the server specified in the Preferences. ?

Summary: HDL Code with default 'Fully Parallel' architecture is not generated if you generate code for any other architecture first.
Description: The coder does not generate HDL code with 'Fully Parallel' architecture if you first generate HDL code for any non-default architecture (for example,  'Fully Serial' or 'Distributed Arithmetic'). The coder continues to generate HDL code with the most recent non-default architecture set by you.
Workaround: Close the Generate HDL dialog box. Re-open the dialog box with the same filter and generate HDL code with default 'Fully Parallel' architecture.

Summary: Simscape system not connected to Simulink blocks could be excluded from simulation
Description: Simscape systems simulated using local solver and not connected to Simulink sinks or output ports by PS-Simulink Converter blocks are sometimes excluded from simulation. In such case, states associated with the Simscape system are not included in the state vector reported by Simulink, and Simscape logging information is not produced.
Workaround: To prevent this issue, disable Simulink block reduction by clearing the Block reduction check box in the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Cannot load a neural network created in version 5.1 or earlier
Description: Loading from a MAT file a neural network that was created in version 5.1 or earlier, results in this error:  Warning: An error occurred when running a class's loadobj method. The object that was loaded from the MAT-file was a copy of the object before the loadobj method was run. The rest of the variables were also loaded from the MAT-file. The encountered error was: Reference to non-existent field 'name'.
Workaround: To work around this error, use the fixed version of +nnupdate/net.m from the attached zip file. Put the fixed version in your current workspace folder as: workspace_folder/+nnupdate/net.m, or replace the original file: matlab/toolbox/nnet/nnutils/+nnupdate/net.m.

Summary: Generation of cosimulation blocks fails
Description: When you try to generate cosimulation blocks for any filter object, using either the Filter Design HDL Coder user interface or the generatehdl command, the coder generates the HDL Code correctly but fails during generation of the cosimulation blocks with this error:### Starting generation of EDA Simulator Link cosimulation blocks.??? Undefined function or method 'generatetbstimulus' for input arguments of type 'filter_type '
Workaround: Generate a cosimulation model and use the cosimulation block that is generated with the model.Via the user interface:Select the Test Bench tabSelect 'Cosimulation model for use with' and select the appropriate HDL simulator from the drop down boxClick GenerateVia the command line:Use the following command for your filter object 'myfilt':generatehdl(myfilt, 'GenerateCosimmodel', 'modelsim')

Summary: Verification stops at the beginning of compilation with the message: "Command line error: -align [16 | 32] is expected for -target x86_64"
Description: When launching a verification with the option -target x86_64  and -align 64 , the verification stops with the following message: Command line error: -align [16 | 32] is expected for -target x86_64.| Launch polyspace-c -h to get help  This happens when we try to force an allignment of 64  with this target.
Workaround: Relaunch the verification without the -align option.64 is the default alignment for the target x86_64, so you do not need to use the -align option.

Summary: Clipping can occur in Windows metafile (emf) images of Simulink and Stateflow diagrams
Description: Clipping can occur in Windows metafile (emf) images of Simulink and Stateflow diagrams produced by the MATLAB print and sfprint commands, respectively. The clipping cuts off a small portion of the top and right side of the image. This clipping occurs when you uncheck the MATLAB preference: Figure Copy Template -> Copy Options -> Match figure screen size .
Workaround: From the MATLAB desktop menu bar, select File->Preferences.In the MATLAB Preferences dialog box, expand the Figure Copy Template node.In the Copy Options panel, select the Match figure screen size option.Click OK to apply the setting and dismiss the preferences dialog box.

Summary: Performance issues when uploading comments to Polyspace Metrics Web server
Description: When you open results from Polypace Metrics, justify a check, and then save the results, the process of saving comments to the Web server can take a long time. The performance issues are most apparant with very large results. From Metrics: Click on a check to review's link to open results in PVE. Justify only 1 check. Click on the Save button to save justifications in Metrics.  The saving time can be 1 or 2 minutes. ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code for Unit Delay operating on a bus signal with frame-based elements
Description: If a model has a Unit Delay block operating on a bus signal with one or more frame-based elements in the bus, the generated code can result in incorrect results.

Summary: Polyspace Verification Environment (Graphical Interface) can raise activation windows when you reach the maximum number of users
Description: The Polyspace GUI opens "Polyspace Software Activation" pop-up windows in two cases: When you need to activate the software When all the keys from your license server have been checked out  When the message concerns the maximum number of users, you do not need to perform activation. ?
Workaround: Click Cancel, and try using the graphical interface later.

Summary: Misleading status message for result folders associated with unit-by-unit verification
Description: When you start a unit-by-unit verification, then switch from one results folder to another in the Project Browser, the results folder for the the unit-by-unit verification incorrectly shows its status as "Completed " instead of "Unit by unit queued on server ." ?

Summary: eig with one input returns eigenvectors that are not normalized.
Description: The MATLAB documentation for eig  says " For eig (A), the eigenvectors are scaled so that the norm of each is 1.0." The MATLAB Coder software does not perform this scaling. This bug affects C/C++ code generated from MATLAB algorithms and from MATLAB Function blocks in Simulink.
Workaround: No action is necessary unless your application requires normalized eigenvectors. If normalized eigenvectors are required, after[V,D] = eig(A);dofor k = 1:size(V,2)  V(:,k) = V(:,k)/norm(V(:,k));end

Summary: solve finds only one solution for a system of equations that has more than one solution
Description: If a system of non-algebraic equations has more than one, but a finite number of solutions, and these solutions are expressible in closed form, solve  sometimes returns only one of these solutions. (Note that for a system that has infinitely many solutions or solutions that can only be computed numerically, this behavior of solve  is intended.)

Summary: Using the three-output form of ldl with the vector option will crash MATLAB? when the input is empty.
Description: Using the three-output form of ldl  with the vector option will crash MATLAB? when the input is empty. For example, the following code: [L,D,p] = ldl([],'vector'); will cause MATLAB to crash.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Behavior of code generated for merged outputs of exported AUTOSAR runnables may not match behavior of Normal mode simulation
Description: If you apply a Merge  block to the outputs of function-call subsystems being exported as AUTOSAR runnables, then the behavior of generated code may not match the behavior of the Normal mode simulation. This mismatch in behavior: Applies to both the SIL simulation and real-time application Can occur if the nonvirtual source of at least one signal entering the Merge  block is inside a Stateflow chart, MATLAB function block, Model block, or S-Function block that conditionally computes that signal, and lies inside one of the function-call subsystems being exported
Workaround: Identify which signals entering the Merge block are conditionally computed by their nonvirtual sources located inside conditionally executed subsystems. For each of these signals:Place a Signal Conversion block on the signal as it enters the outport of the function-call subsystem from which an AUTOSAR runnable is exported.Double-click the block. The Block Parameters dialog box opens.Select the check box Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization.Click OK.Note that this workaround may change the functionality of the model.

Summary: To File block uses incorrect decimation for accelerated simulation targets
Description: The decimation is applied twice by the To File block in the following targets: Simulink Accelerator Rapid Accelerator Model Reference Accelerator RSim  For example, if the decimation parameter is set to 2, the actual decimation will be 4, and if the decimation parameter is set to 3, the actual decimation will be 9. ?

Summary: Simulink XML Comparison may not show changes in Signal Builder blocks and other blocks that create "MATStruct" data
Description: The Simulink XML Comparison feature of the Simulink Report Generator defines all changes to "MATStruct" properties of the XML files as nonfunctional changes. This means that they are not shown by default in the XML Comparison report. Functional changes in Signal Builder blocks are not shown by default, because they store information in MATStruct tags. Some other information may be stored in MATStruct tags, depending on the type of data that is being stored. For example, any blocks that have persistent UserData associated with them, or any block diagram to which the user has added Metadata.
Workaround: To work around this problem, after you have reviewed all the functional changes within your design, turn off the filter to hide nonfunctional changes by selecting Comparison > Filter and clear the check box for filtering "Nonfunctional changes".Select Edit > Find in the Comparison report to search for instances of MATStruct within the report. Find and review all MATStruct changes within Signal Builder blocks, and any other parts of the XML file that include MATStruct tags.

Summary: MEX documentation examples using LAPACK or BLAS functions produce unpredictable results when built without using -largeArrayDims.
Description: Because the MATLAB mwlapack  and mwblas  libraries only support 64-bit integers for matrix dimensions, you must use the mex  build script -largeArrayDims  option for MEX files that link to LAPACK or BLAS. Otherwise, these MEX files produce unpredictable results, including potentially crashing MATLAB. The examples that use LAPACK or BLAS are: dotProductComplex.c matrixDivide.c matrixDivideComplex.c matrixMultiply.c utdu_slv.c
Workaround: Build all MEX-files that use LAPACK or BLAS with the -largeArrayDims flag.

Summary: Code generation error for variant subsystems using preprocessor conditionals and linked Stateflow charts or MATLAB Function blocks
Description: You might get an error when generating code for a model with a variant subsystem that: Generates preprocessor conditionals Contains a Stateflow chart or MATLAB Function block that is a library link  The error looks something like this: Error reported by S-function 'sf_sfun' in 'VariantSubsystem/VariantSubsystem/GainScheduleVariant2/GainSchedule/ SFunction ':Error calling generated SFunction, VariantSubsystem_sfun.
Workaround: Disable the library link for the Stateflow chart or MATLAB Function block inside the variant subsystem that generates preprocessor conditionals.

Summary: Compiler warning or error for code generated from a subsystem connected to a root outport
Description: Compiling code generated for a subsystem generates a warning or error if all of the following conditions exist: The subsystem is configured to generate a nonreusable function. The subsystem is conditionally executed. For example, the subsystem is a triggered subsystem. The subsystem is connected to a root outport of the containing model. The containing model has been configured using Configure Model Functions  on the Interface  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, such that the root outport to which the subsystem is connected is specified as the return argument of the model step function.  Under these conditions, compiling the generated subsystem code generates warning or error messages that might indicate type or pointer mismatches, for example,  "assignment from incompatible pointer type ".

Summary: MATLAB crashes when using a DALSA? frame grabber with the Image Acquisition Toolbox? and Sapera 7
Description: When using a DALSA frame grabber with the Image Acquisition Toolbox and Sapera 7, the toolbox is able to detect the hardware and create a videoinput  object, but is unable to acquire images from the frame grabber. Using the preview , getsnapshot , or start  commands on the videoinput object will cause MATLAB to crash. This crash is caused by incorrect property constraints on the source object of the videoinput  object. In some instances, the property constraints are not being initialized correctly, which leads to the crash. To determine if this issue is causing your crash, use the following reproduction steps: % Customize the following two values for your application: deviceID = 1;camFile = C:\DALSA\Sapera\CamFiles\User\camfile.ccfvid = videoinput('dalsa', deviceID, camFile);propinf(src, 'StrobeDelay')ans =                Type: 'double'         Constraint: 'bounded'    ConstraintValue: [0 -2]       DefaultValue: 0           ReadOnly: 'never'     DeviceSpecific: 1 This will display the property information for the StrobeDelay property. The field of interest is the ConstraintValue  field. The first value is the minimum allowable value, and the second is the maximum. If the second value is less than the first, MATLAB will crash when an acquisition is started. In this case the constraint values of [0 -2]  are a symptom of this bug.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Extract the files from the .zip file. Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Enumerations defined using Simulink.defineIntEnumType might cause MATLAB to crash if they are used in a MATLAB function block.
Description: Enumerations used in a MATLAB Function block might cause MATLAB to crash during model simulation under these conditions: Enumeration is defined using Simulink.defineIntEnumType  AddClassNameToEnumNames  property is set to true
Workaround: If the AddClassNameToEnumNames property must be true, define the enumeration in MATLAB code, rather than using Simulink.defineIntEnumType. For example:classdef(Enumeration) Colors < Simulink.IntEnumType enumeration   Blue(1),   Red(2),   Green(4) end methods (Static = true)    function retVal = addClassNameToEnumNames()     retVal = true;   end  endend

Summary: linprog fails to detect infeasibility when "fixed" variables are inconsistent with equality constraints.
Description: Under the following conditions, the default linprog  algorithm returns an incorrect solution, and fails to state that the problem is infeasible:* All variables present in linear constraints have finite upper bounds that are equal to their lower bounds, i.e., they are "fixed."* All linear constraints are equalities.* These linear equality constraints are inconsistent with respect to fixed variables.
