Summary: Cannot generate a new coverage report when there is an existing read-only report.
Description: When there is an existing read-only simulation coverage report, if you try to generate a new report, you see the following error: Error in ==> ReportUtils.get_report_file_name>unique_file_name_using_numbers at 26  if (any(baseName(end)=='0123456789')) ??? Undefined function or variable "baseName".
Workaround: Delete the read-only file.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: If "Use integer division to handle net slopes that are reciprocals of integers" is on, a conversion between two fixed-point data types with different slopes and biases might cause a mismatch between simulation and code generation
Description: When performing a conversion between two fixed-point data types with mismatched slopes and biases, the simulation results might not provide bit-true agreement with the code generation results. Code generation provides the intended behavior. This problem can occur only under certain circumstances. The necessary conditions include the following: The "Use integer division to handle net slopes that are reciprocals of integers" option on the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box is selected. The two fixed-point data types have different biases. The two fixed-point data types have different slopes. The net slope correction is not a power of two. The net slope correction can be accurately represented as an integer reciprocal such as 1/30. The rounding mode specified for the cast matches the "Signed integer division rounds to" parameter setting configured for the embedded hardware on the Hardware Implementation pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.  Simulation incorrectly implements the decision logic to apply the "Use integer division to handle net slopes that are reciprocals of integers" optimization. Simulation incorrectly rules out all data type conversions involving mismatched bias.
Workaround: A workaround is to split the data type conversion into two conversions. In one conversion, change the bias, but not the slope. In the other conversion, change the slope, but not the bias. You can do these conversions in either order.

Summary: HDL code from FFT block in multi-rate models might not match Simulink result
Description: When your model contains multiple sample rates, and one of the rates is not an integer multiple of the rate that the FFT block is running, the generated HDL code might not match the Simulink result. An example of this scenario is when an FFT block has a sample rate of 2.5e-008 s, and another block or subsystem has a sample rate of 3.7879e-010 s in the same model.

Summary: Unnecessary code generated for subsystem enable or trigger signal for which the data type cannot represent zero
Description: If a signal drives the enable or trigger port of a subsystem and its data type cannot represent zero, code incorrectly is generated to detect a zero-crossing. For example, if a trigger signal is an unsigned fixed-point type with positive bias, the real-world value of the signal is always positive. If you use this signal as a trigger, there should never be a zero crossing. However, the generated code unnecessarily compares the stored integer value against 0U to determine if there is a zero crossing.

Summary: Code generation results differ from simulation results when Probe block input is a variable-size signal with size or dimensions greater than range of selected output data type
Description: When a variable-size signal is input to a Probe block, there can be a difference between simulation and code-generation results for the "Probe width" and "Probe signal dimensions" outputs. Such a difference is due to an input width or input signal dimension being large enough to cause an overflow. In particular, this bug occurs when both the following conditions are true:  The input to a Probe block is a variable-size signal. The input signal width cannot be represented within the range of the data type selected for the width output, or the input signal dimension cannot be represented within the range of the data type selected for the signal dimension output.
Workaround: This bug occurs due to a difference in overflow and rounding modes between simulation and generated code for the Probe block. It manifests itself  for variable-size signal inputs. To detect whether this bug occurs in your model, extract and run the attached detection script as follows:detectProbeOverflowRoundingBug('modelname') The script returns 'true' if the bug manifests itself in your model. To work around this issue, change the selected data type for width and signal dimensions to 'double' and insert 'Data Type Conversion' blocks at the output with the following parameters (use default values for unspecified parameters): Output data type: [Your desired type]Integer rounding mode: NearestSaturate on integer overflow: on

Summary: Cannot save a model containing Chinese characters
Description: If you try to save a model which uses the "GBK" character encoding and contains any non-ASCII characters, Simulink throws the following error and does not save the model: Error: The model 'mymodel' contains characters that cannot be encoded with the model's character encoding, 'GBK'. Either:1) change the character encoding setting of the model, by using: set_param('mymodel', 'SavedCharacterEncoding', 'Windows-1252'), or2) identify the characters that cannot be encoded and remove them from the model. The first unsupported character occurs on line 999, byte offset 66 in the file mymodel.err The error message is incorrect to say the characters cannot be encoded in GBK. The first suggested solution enables you to save the model but causes other problems, such as garbled HTML reports generated by the Requirements Management feature. The second suggested solution is impractical, since it will require the removal of all non-ASCII characters. This bug occurs when saving any model which contains non-ASCII characters and uses a character encoding other than one of the five fully-supported ones: Windows-1252, ISO-8859-1, Shift-JIS, UTF-8, US-ASCII.
Workaround: The only complete workaround is to use only ASCII characters in the model.  A partial workaround is to change the character encoding of the model as suggested above, so that it uses a fully-supported encoding (e.g. Windows-1252) in which the byte sequences in the file are likely to be valid.  However, be aware that when generating HTML reports (e.g. with the Requirements Management feature), this may result in mojibake - garbled sequences of characters. See: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mojibake

Summary: video.MultimediaFileWriter System object and To Multimedia File block cannot write multiple wmv files
Description: An error is generated when trying to write wmv files. This error occurs when any one of the following is true: Two or more instances of the video.MultimediaFileWriter  System object are used in a loop. Two or more instances of the signalblks.MultimediaFileWriter  System object are used in a loop. Two or more of the To Multimedia File  blocks are used in a model. The user does not have write permission to the C drive.
Workaround: To workaround this problem for R2010b version of MATLAB?, follow these steps for your specific platform:1. Quit MATLAB?.2. Backup the following file: For 32 bit platforms: $MATLABROOT\bin\win32\sldirectshow.dll For 64 bit platforms: $MATLABROOT\bin\win64\sldirectshow.dll3. Download the attached file attachment_682465_20101207.zip and unzip it. Rename and save the following file: For 32 bit platforms: sldirectshow.dll_32 to $MATLABROOT\bin\win32\sldirectshow.dll For 64 bit platforms: sldirectshow.dll_64 to $MATLABROOT\bin\win64\sldirectshow.dll4. Restart MATLAB?.5. Execute the "rehash toolbox" command.

Summary: The Environment Controller block may error when non-bus signals are connected to it
Description: The following error appears when both of the conditions described below apply: The Signal Conversion block 'modelname/Environment Controller/Numerical Unity' cannot operate on a non-bus signal when it has been configured to expect a bus signal. Consider setting the Output parameter of the block to 'Contiguous copy'. That error message appears when both of these conditions are true: The diagnostic Configuration Parameters -> Diagnostics -> Connectivity -> Non-bus signals treated as bus signals  is set to error . A nonbus signal is connected to the inputs of the Environment Controller block.
Workaround: The workaround is to use a modified version of the Environment Controller block with non-bus signals.To accomplish this:Copy the Environment Controller block from the Simulink library (Signal Routing) into a new library.Rename the block to "Environmental Controller for non-bus Inputs".Right-click the block and choose Link Options -> Disable Link.Right click the block again and choose Link Options -> Break Link.Right-click the block once more and choose Look Under Mask.Double-click the Numerical Unity block and in the Output parameter, choose the Contiguous Copy optionClick OK to close the dialog box.Save the library in a convenient place on the MATLAB path.Use this library version of the block (that is, the Environmental Controller for non-bus Inputs block) whenever you need to use the block with non-bus signals.Use the shipping version of the block in the Simulink -> Signal Routing library when you need to use the block with bus signals.

Summary: Generated code build failure with xPC Target on 64-bit platform
Description: Code compilation fails for xPC Target on 64-bit platforms when you compile code generated from a Simulink model that contains Simscape-based blocks.

Summary: Publishing to PDF returns errors in MATLAB? R2010b and R2011a
Description: If you try to publish to PDF, then MATLAB 2010b returns errors if the Report Generator is not installed, and MATLAB R2011a returns errors under all circumstances. MATLAB R2010b returns these errors:  ??? Undefined variable "org" or class "org.apache.fop.apps.Driver". Error in ==> publish>publishToPdf at 501 fopDriver = org.apache.fop.apps.Driver; Error in ==> publish at 193  MATLAB R2011a returns these errors:  ??? Undefined variable "rptgen" or class "rptgen.file2urn".Error in ==> publish>fopInitialize at 548fopFactory.setHyphenBaseURL(rptgen.file2urn( ...Error in ==> publish>publishToPdf at 501[fopDriver, fopOutputStream] = fopInitialize(outputAbsoluteFilename);Error in ==> publish at 193        publishToPdf(dom,options,outputAbsoluteFilename)
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ¡°Error: Access is denied¡± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ¡°WinZip¡± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ¡°Run as administrator¡±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation for Weighted Sample Time Math block inside a triggered subsystem under certain conditions
Description: Incorrect code is generated for the output of a Weighted Sample Time Math block when the following conditions hold: The Weighted Sample Time Math block is within a triggered subsystem The block's dialog box parameter Operation  is set to *  (multiply) or /  (divide) The data type of input to the block has a non-zero bias
Products: MATLAB Coder, Simulink Coder, Embedded Coder

Summary: Incorrect result for repeated Software-In-The-Loop (SIL) simulations
Description: An auto-generated S-function, used to validate generated C code for a Subsystem block, might not reset its' states at the start of simulation under the following conditions: On the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box of the parent model, the Remove internal data zero initialization  parameter is selected. The subsystem contains at least one Model block.  The simulation results of a repeated simulation are incorrect because the internal states of the auto-generated S-function contain the last values from the previous simulation run.
Workaround: You can explicitly reset the states of the auto-generated S-function using the following command at the MATLAB command line>> clear $SubSystem_Name$_sf$SubSystem_Name$ is the name of the Subsystem block used to generate the S-function for SIL verification.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Dot Product block might return different results in normal mode simulation and code generation for unsigned complex inputs
Description: The Dot Product block might return different results in normal mode simulation and code generation when the following conditions are true: The block uses complex inputs of unsigned integer type. The input values cause internal saturation.

Summary: On Windows Vista, or Windows 7 with Aero features turned OFF, certain blocks may throw an error to the MATLAB command prompt when drawing.
Description: On Windows Vista, or Windows 7 with Aero features turned OFF, certain blocks (for example, Variable Transport Delay) may display the following error message in the MATLAB command prompt when drawn: "Windows returned error message: Not enough storage is available to process this command."  The issue is caused by the graphics system in Windows Vista and Windows 7, and the size of the block icon. This error happens when the icon gets small enough.
Workaround: Increase the size of the block icon for any block that causes this error.Alternatively, you can use a a different zoom level to view the model.

Summary: Simulink structure parameter with nonfinite field might cause uncompilable code
Description: If a model references a structure parameter, where a field value is nonfinite, the code generator might produce uncompilable code. The code generation failure might appear as follows: 'rtInf' undeclared here (not in a function) gmake: *** [modelname_data.o] Error 1 ### Build procedure for model: 'modelname' aborted due to an error.  This issue also affects the simulation that requires code generation.
Workaround: Remove the field, containing nonfinite value, from the structure parameter and store it in a separate non-structure parameter.

Summary: When you do not provide gradients, the fmincon interior-point and sqp algorithms fail to internally scale your problem.
Description: When you set the ScaleProblem  option to 'obj-and-constr' , fmincon  should internally scale the objective and constraints. Because of a bug, in the cases when you do not provide gradients, the scaling does not take place. Specifically, when ScaleProblem  is 'obj-and-constr' and the Algorithm  option is interior-point  or sqp  :* If you do not provide the objective function gradient (GradObj  is 'off' ), fmincon  does not scale the objective function.* If you do not provide the nonlinear constraint gradients (GradConstr  is 'off ' ), fmincon  does not scale the nonlinear constraint functions. * fmincon  always scales any linear constraints. The other fmincon  algorithms (active-set  and trust-region-reflective ) do not have this problem, since they do not support the ScaleProblem  option. When you provide gradients for the objective function and any nonlinear constraint functions, fmincon  performs the scaling as expected.

Summary: The fopen function does not properly resolve a relative path containing "..".
Description: The fopen  function does not properly resolve a relative path containing ".. ". For example, the following command should return a handle to a file text.txt  in the parent folder. Instead, it returns text.txt  in the current folder:    f = fopen('.\..\text.txt', 'r');

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Lookup Table Dynamic block output might differ in simulation and code generation for some input values when input port x or xdat is single
Description: When input port x and/or xdat of the Lookup Table Dynamic block is of single  data type, block output from simulation and code generation might not match for some specific values. Here is an example: Suppose that a and b are two values that can be represented using single  data type. However, (a - b) cannot be represented using single  data type. Here are cases where this block generates different results in simulation and code generation. Case 1: Suppose that xdat is [a, b]. Then the value mismatch can happen. Case 2: Suppose that xdat is [a, c], where c is another finite number. And input x is b. Then the value mismatch can happen too.
Workaround: Change both data types of the input x and input xdat to double.

Summary: On Windows 7, compiling MEX-files using OpenWatcom might generate the error "Compiler returned a bad status".
Description: On Windows 7, if the MATLAB installation path contains a space, for example: C:\Program Files\MATLAB  compiling a MEX-file using the OpenWatcom compiler generates the following error: Error! E1073: Invalid option '-I"C:\Program' Error! E1139: Command line contains more than one file to compile Unable to open 'Files\MATLAB\R2010b"\extern\include.c' Error: Compiler returned a bad status compiling "<filename>"

Summary: Timeout error may occur in drawnow or figure commands executed while previewing a video
Description: When running a GUI that includes a video preview window, you may occasionally receive a timeout error while executing either drawnow  or figure  command. The error looks similar to the following: ??? Error using ==> figure UIJ_AreThereWindowShowsPending - timeout waiting for window to show up

Summary: Closing a model after deleting a physical modeling subsystem can crash MATLAB
Description: Closing a model after deleting a physical modeling subsystem can crash MATLAB. This can occur if the subsystem contains a labeled connection line.
Workaround: Delete the contents of the subsystem.Save the model.Delete the subsystem.Save the model.Close the model.

Summary: Publishing to Microsoft Word? returns errors
Description: MATLAB? publishing to Microsoft? Word returns errors when Microsoft Word 2010 is installed.You might see an error similar to:??? Undefined function or method 'get_param' for input arguments of type 'Interface.Microsoft_Word_14.0_Object_Library._Document'.  Or you might see an error similar to:??? Error using ==> saveas at 72 Invalid Simulink object specifier.
Workaround: Edit the following file, where MATLABROOT is the folder returned when you type matlabroot in the MATLAB Command Window:  MATLABROOT/toolbox/matlab/codetools/private/mxdom2word.mSearch for these lines,try    doc.SaveAs(outputPath,wdFormatDocument);catch anErrorand replace them with these lines:try    try        % This will work on Word 2010.        doc.SaveAs2(outputPath,wdFormatDocument);    catch %#ok<CTCH>        doc.SaveAs(outputPath,wdFormatDocument);    endcatch anError

Summary: If a MuPAD input region contains a command that follows reset(), not all notebook settings are available for that command.
Description: After reset() , the notebook reinitializes the MuPAD kernel. During initialization, MuPAD sends the current notebook settings, such as Typeset Math, Abbreviate Output, the name and path of the notebook (defined by the variables NOTEBOOKFILE  and NOTEBOOKPATH ), initialization scripts, and other settings, to the kernel. If you put any commands in the same input region after reset() , the system executes these commands before it completes initialization.For example, if you create the function call produceSomeFancyOutput()  and put it in the same region after reset() , you will get the output in ASCII PrettyPrint. This is true even if the notebook setting 'Typeset Math' is activated. The reason is that the kernel default setting after reset() is ASCII PrettyPrint: reset():produceSomeFancyOutput()   The following commands placed in the same input region produce an error. The reason is that the NOTEBOOKPATH  variable is not defined when you call read: reset():read(NOTEBOOKPATH."to_be_loaded.mu")
Workaround: Use a separate input region to call reset().

Summary: Simulink stops responding when you connect an ActiveX Control block to an ActiveX control located in a MATLAB figure created using GUIDE.
Description: Simulink stops responding when you connect an ActiveX Control block to an ActiveX control created using GUIDE. This behavior might occur when you configure the traditional ActiveX control block as an off block and connecting it to display the Simulink signal output in the ActiveX control located in a MATLAB figure.
Workaround: As a workaround, you can programatically create an ActiveX control inside a MATLAB figure instead of creating one with GUIDE. For example, create and execute a MATLAB program that contains these statements:f = figure;h = actxcontrol('mwagauge.agaugectrl.1',[100 100 100 100],f);This MATLAB program creates a figure window that contains a Generic Angular Gauge whose program ID is mwagauge.agaugectrl.??

Summary: resample and upfirdn functions generate segmentation violations with very large irreducible resampling factors
Description: In upfirdn  and resample , if the ratio of the interpolation factor to the decimation factor, P/Q, is very large with no common factors, the pointer to the buffer containing the filter states may overflow causing a segmentation violation. This issue is more likely to be observed in 32-bit than 64-bit architectures. The value of P/Q required to cause a pointer overflow in 64-bit architectures is so large that it is unlikely to be practical for any application.

Summary: Error calling Curve Fitting Toolbox function from a deployed component
Description: I have two MATLAB compiler generated C++ shared libraries --- one of them calls Curve Fitting Toolbox functions and the other one calls magic . If I initialize the library that calls magic  first, I get the following errors when running the driver application: ??? Undefined variable "curvefit" or class "curvefit.llsqoptions". Error in ==> fitoptions at 192 Error in ==> liblookup>iPolynomialSurfaceLookup at 339 Error in ==> liblookup at 157 Error in ==> fittype.fittype>fittype.fittype at 243 Error in ==> fittest at 7 Undefined variable "curvefit" or class "curvefit.llsqoptions". Error in =>fittest.m at 7  If I initialize the library calling Curve Fitting Toolbox function before the magicsquare  library, my driver app works perfectly without any errors. The order of the library Terminate()  functions does not matter, only the order of the calls to the library Initialize()  functions make a difference.
Workaround: To work around this issue do one of the following:Compile all the MATLAB functions into one component.Initialize the component containing the Curve Fitting Toolbox functionality first.

Summary: For HTML output, MATLAB Property Tables do not control table borders on individual cells
Description: The MATLAB Property Table component controls border properties of individual cells for PDF, RTF and DOC output formats. However, HTML formats do not honor the border properties of individual cells, and instead properties apply to all borders of a table. ?
Workaround: Use the Custom Stylesheet tool provided with Report Generator:Create a custom stylesheet for use with HTML.Add the Table Borders With Css to it and set its value to 1.Use this new custom stylesheet with the corresponding report.This procedure allows you to configure HTML table borders on a cell-by-cell basis.Learn more about Custom Stylesheets here: https://www.mathworks.com/help/toolbox/rptgen/ug/bqad9fi-1.html

Summary: Camera toolbar tools work incorrectly with very small or large axes limits
Description: When using the camera toolbar to manipulate an axes whose limits are either very large or very small, the Move Camera Forward/Back  tool on the camera toolbar does not behave as expected. The following code illustrates the issue: [x,y] = meshgrid([-1e6:10000:1e6],[-1e6:10000:1e6]);surf(x,y,abs(x)+abs(y));cameratoolbar show If you select the Move Camera Forward/Back  button, mouse gestures in the axes have no effect.

Summary: Syntactically incorrect minimum or maximum value generated in ASAP2 file for Simulink data object with unspecified minimum or maximum value
Description: The ASAP2 file generation process might generate a syntactically incorrect minimum or maximum value for a Simulink data object if the minimum or maximum value is not specified for the data object. For example, if you create a Simulink.Parameter  object, with Data type  uint8  and no specified minimum value, the ASAP2 file generation process generates a minimum value described as MIN_uint8_T . Note:  You can specify minimum and maximum values by entering values for Minimum  and Maximum  in the data object dialog box, or by setting the data object properties Min  and Max  at the command line.

Summary: In the Progress Monitor window of the Polyspace Queue Manager (Spooler), the computation time can decrease as a verification runs instead of increasing.
Description: When the time on the server running a verification is ahead of the time on the client, the computation time displayed in the Progress Monitor will decrease as a verification runs. For example, this can happen if the client is in one time zone, and the server is in another time zone.

Summary: xlabel causes subplot to rescale all axes
Description: Execute the following:  for i = 1 : 6    subplot(6, 1, i);    plot(0 : 1, 0 : 1);end ? When we execute the following:  xlabel('Time') the height of each subplot axes has rescaled.

Summary: The function CELINTENSITYREAD returns arbitrary intensity values for unpaired probes.
Description: The function CELINTENSITYREAD  returns arbitrary intensity values for probes that are unpaired on some Affymetrix GeneChip arrays. In some CDF libraries for Affymetrix GeneChip arrays (for example exon arrays), some unpaired probes are "paired with" the position (0,0) on the array. The intensity value at (0,0) is arbitary and should not be considered as the mismatch (MM) or perfect match (PM) pair for a given probe.

Summary: Invoking Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct with a bus object can result in an error about under-specified data types
Description: You may encounter the following error when invoking Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct  with a bus object name: ??? Error using ==> Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct at 61 Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct uses a temporary model 'TmpModelForCreatingMATLABStruct' to create a MATLAB struct. An error occurred while creating the struct. Please see the following causes: Caused by: Error using ==> Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct>compileModelAndGetStruct at 231 Error due to multiple causes. Error using ==> Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct>compileModelAndGetStruct at 231 The data types for signals in this model are under-specified. Simulink is using a heuristic to select the data type for output port 1 of 'TmpModelForCreatingMATLABStruct/In2'. The heuristic leads to reasonable choices in most cases. Usage of the heuristic can be avoided by setting signal data types using Signal Specification blocks or signal objects Error using ==> Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct>compileModelAndGetStruct at 231 The data types for signals in this model are under-specified. Simulink is using a heuristic to select the data type for input port 1 of 'TmpModelForCreatingMATLABStruct/Out'. The heuristic leads to reasonable choices in most cases. Usage of the heuristic can be avoided by setting signal data types using Signal Specification blocks or signal objects
Workaround: Install the appropriate attached .zip file:For R2010a, install attachment_630310_10a_2011-06-29.zip.For R2010b or R2011a, install attachment_630310_10b_11a_2011-06-29.zip.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ¡°Error: Access is denied¡± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ¡°WinZip¡± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ¡°Run as administrator¡±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: On a Linux 64-bit system, Polyspace installation stops with the message 'exec: 2433: absolutepath/Linux/resource/jre/bin/java: not found'
Description: When installing PolySpace products on a 64-bit Linux system, the installation process ends with the following error message: exec: 2433: <absolutePath>/Linux/resource/jre/bin/java: not found  The complete output of the installation process is: In stallation Procedure (c) 1999-2009, The MathWorks, Inc.  Launching the installer...  Preparing to install...  Extracting the JRE from the installer archive...  Unpacking the JRE...  Extracting the installation resources from the installer archive...  Configuring the installer for this system's environment...  awk: error while loading shared libraries: libdl.so.2: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory  ...  It could also be the following:...Launching installer...exec: 2433: /tmp/install.dir.880/Linux/resource/jre/bin/java: not found  An error has occured and some files have not been installed.Please check free disk space (almost 600MB are required to install all products).Look at the log file for details.In case of trouble with the main installer or java, you can try to relaunch this script with -console.  This error occurs because the Polyspace installer is a 32-bit binary using 32-bit binaries, which may cause problems on a 64-bit machine. It needs a 32-bit library installed.
Workaround: The Polyspace installer needs 32-bit library installed.To work around this issue on a 64-bit Ubuntu Linux machine, install the package "ia32-libs" from your Linux distribution: apt-get install ia32-libsNote: On other Linux releases, the command can be different. On a Red Hat 64-bit Linux machine, for example, you can perform this installation by executing the following command in a shell: yum install glibc-devel.i386

Summary: Exported functions PIL block simulation causes MATLAB and Simulink to fail
Description: A PIL block created by an exported functions build will cause MATLAB and Simulink to fail during simulation if the following are true:  The top-level virtual subsystem comprises a direct connection between an inport and outport  There are no function-call subsystems inside the top-level virtual subsystem.
Workaround: The scenario where the top-level virtual subsystem contains no function-call subsystems does not represent a valid exported functions workflow. To use the exported functions workflow, the top-level virtual subsystem must contain function-call subsystems.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Passing the output of coder.nullcopy (formerly eml.nullcopy) to a function without initializing it first might result in unpredictable behavior
Description: You can declare uninitialized variables or structure fields using coder.nullcopy . However, you must initialize such a variable before passing it to a function or otherwise using it in a computation. Otherwise, you might get compilation errors or generate incorrect code.  See documentation on coder.nullcopy  for details.
Workaround: Explicitly initialize variables or structure fields before using them. For example, the following code is incorrect: x = coder.nullcopy(zeros(3,3));y = sin(x); The following code corrects the problem by initializing x before passing it to the sin function: x = eml.nullcopy(zeros(3,3));for i = 1:9   if mod(i,2) == 0      x(i) = i;   else       x(i) = 0;   endendy = sin(x);      ?

Summary: int fails to compute the definite integral representing the area of an ellipse
Description: In Symbolic Math Toolbox 5.1 (R2008b) and 5.2 (R2009a), int  fails to compute the definite integral representing the area of an ellipse with semi-axes of lengths a and b. For example, the following commands syms x a bint(2*b*sqrt(1-x^2/a^2), x, -a, a)  return an unresolved integral: Warning: Explicit integral could not be found.> In sym.int at 64ans =int(2*b*(1 - x^2/a^2)^(1/2), x = -a..a)  Simplification of that unresolved integral returns an incorrect result: simplify(ans)ans =-pi*a*b  In Symbolic Math Toolbox 5.3 (R2009b), int  returns a correct, but unexpectedly long result: syms x a bint(2*b*sqrt(1-x^2/a^2), x, -a, a)  ans =(-2)*b*(limit((a*atan(x/(a*(1 - x^2/a^2)^(1/2))))/2 + (x*(- x^2/a^2 + 1)^(1/2))/2, x = -a, Right) - limit(1/2*a*atan(1/a*x/(1 - 1/a^2*x^2)^(1/2)) + 1/2*x*(1 - 1/a^2*x^2)^(1/2), x = a, Left))  Simplification does not help to get the expected result: simplify(ans)ans =-a*b*i*(log(i/(2/a)^(1/2)) - log(-i/(2/a)^(1/2)))
Workaround: In Symbolic Math Toolbox 5.1 (R2008b) and 5.2 (R2009a), explicitly declare that a and b are positive:syms xsyms a b positiveint(2*b*sqrt(1-x^2/a^2), x, -a, a)Warning: Explicit integral could not be found. In sym.int at 64ans =int(2*b*(1 - x^2/a^2)^(1/2), x = -a..a)Simplify the result:simplify(ans)ans =pi*a*bIn Symbolic Math Toolbox 5.3 (R2009b), explicitly declare that a and b are positive:syms xsyms a b positiveint(2*b*sqrt(1-x^2/a^2), x, -a, a)ans =pi*a*b

Summary: Editing a stylesheet can freeze the Report Explorer
Description: Editing a stylesheet can freeze the Report Explorer. This occurs if you edit a stylesheet element and then switch to another node in the Report Explorer tree, without first saving the stylesheet.
Workaround: After you make a change to a stylesheet element, save the stylesheet before switching to another node in the Report Explorer tree.

Summary: The Simulink Signal and Scope Manager checks out toolbox licenses prematurely
Description: When you open the Signal and Scope Manager, the Signal and Scope Manager displays a list of viewers and generators for all licensed Simulink blocksets.If a Communications Toolbox or Signal Processing Toolbox license is available, the Signal and Scope Manager checks out those licenses. This checking out of licenses is premature, the license checkout for any toolbox should only occur once a viewer or generator from that toolbox is selected from the list.
Workaround: If a user of the Signal and Scope Manager does not need the Communications Toolbox or Signal Processing Toolbox, but a second user needs a license from a shared pool of licenses, the second user can force a license to be checked out.The second user should start his MATLAB session and issue the following commands before the first user opens the Signal and Scope Manager.license('checkout','communication_toolbox')license('checkout','signal_toolbox')

Summary: Function ginput returns different coordinates for keyboard button presses and mouse clicks in MATLAB? 7.6 (R2008a)
Description: The ginput  graphic input function returns different coordinates for keyboard button presses and mouse clicks in MATLAB 7.6 (R2008a).  To see the issue, run the following code: [x1,y1,but1]=ginput(2)  Dock the figure and click somewhere in the figure window, and then press any key on the keyboard. Do not move the mouse between these two steps.  Notice that the coordinates returned in x1  are identical, but the coordinates returned in y1  are different.

Summary: MATLAB can become unresponsive when using the xPC Target explorer and creating figures with legends
Description: when the xPC Target explorer is open, and you create visible figures with plots and a legend, MATLAB can become unresponsive. A target connection is not required for the issue to occur.
Workaround: To work around this issue, disable refreshing in the xPC Target Explorer. Go to the Tools menu and select the Disable Refresh option.

Summary: MCR installer requirements dialog box should say "Click Install".
Description: The MCR installer requirements dialog box instructs you to "Click OK..." but the dialog box does not have an OK button. The dialog box should say "Click Install". ?
Workaround: Ignore the instructions and click the Install button.

Summary: Financial time series objects do not display in the MATLAB? Variable Editor
Description: If you attempt to open a financial time series object in the MATLAB Variable Editor, an empty Variable Editor window with a blank spreadsheet opens.
Workaround: Type the name of the variable in the MATLAB Command Window, and then press Enter. The variable's information displays in the MATLAB Command Window.

Summary: Using netCDF library to write more than 4GB of data to fixed size file fails silently.
Description: Using the netCDF library functions to write more than 4GB of data to a fixed size file fails without issuing an error message. The following example illustrates this issue. ncid = netcdf.create('big.nc','clobber');xlen = 2^18;ylen = 2^16;xdim = netcdf.defDim(ncid,'x',xlen);ydim = netcdf.defDim(ncid,'y',ylen);varid = netcdf.defVar(ncid,'z','byte',[ydim xdim]); % 16 GB variablenetcdf.endDef(ncid);data = ones([ylen xlen],'uint8');netcdf.putVar(ncid,varid,data);netcdf.close(ncid);
Workaround: To workaround this issue, rewrite the code that performs the write operation in the previous example:netcdf.putVar(ncid,varid,data);Use two optional parameters to specify the starting point of the write operation and the number of data elements to write.netcdf.putVar(ncid,varid,[0 0],size(data),data);

Summary: The number of reviewed gray checks never reaches the number of checks to review
Description: When you finish reviewing all checks, the number of checks reviewed should be equal to the number of checks to review in each category. However, even after you review all checks, the number of reviewed checks can be lower than the number of checks to review (as defined by the criterion level you set). This is a bug in Polyspace Client 7.1 (R2009b) in the way the Viewer handles the number of gray checks.

Summary: LaTeX font installation can cause glyphs to be incorrect or missing
Description: Although MATLAB graphics integrates the official releases of TeX and LaTeX, there are some situations in which glyphs rendered on axes, titles, labels are incorrect or missing.
Workaround: Manually install the Computer Modern fonts, which are used by the LaTeX interpreter, according to instructions for your platform.On Windows,Choose Fonts on the Control PanelSelect the menu item File -> Install New Font , and browse to matlabroot/sys/fonts/type1/cm.On Macintosh, choose Applications -> Font Book, and browse to matlabroot/sys/fonts/ttf/cm.On Unix, your system administrator should help you choose the correct font manager application. Browse to matlabroot/sys/fonts/type1/cm.Alternatively, you can explicitly specify characters and fonts manually using the default TeX interpreter. For example,xlabel(['\fontname{courier} Left \fontname{helvetica}',char(176),' \fontname{times} Right'])will draw the word "Left" in Courier, followed by the degree symbol (character 176) in Helvetica, followed by the word "Right" in Times on an axes label.

Summary: Minus sign, left bracket and other symbols do not render on LaTeX text objects
Description: When I give the command  t4 = text(0.2,0.45,'$\Biggl[\biggl[\Bigl[\bigl[$This is a bracket$\bigr]\Bigr]\biggr]\Biggr]$',...     'fontsize',18,'interpreter','latex');  The \biggl  bracket symbol is missing, but the other symbols appear correct. This bug occurs on Linux, Solaris, and Mac but not on Windows. The problem is related to how MATLAB uses the TeX Computer Modern Fonts. TeX font files use non-standard mappings to encode font symbols, causing problems with some operating systems.
Workaround: No workarounds exist for this issue.

Summary: Multiple-line statements might produce incorrect results when following a keyboard function, or might results in problems when opening the Variable Editor
Description: Multiple-line statements might produce problems in these situations. Keyboard Function Issues  If you use the keyboard  function, followed by a multiple-line statement, as in the following examples, you might get incorrect results or MATLAB? might incorrectly report errors. Example 1 - Keyboard Function Issues: >>  keyboardK>> for a=1:5    a    end Example 2 - Keyboard Function Issues: >> keyboardK>> a = ...2 Variable Editor Issues  If you enter the first line of a multiple-line statement, as in the following examples, and then try to open the Variable Editor by double-clicking a variable in the Workspace browser, the Variable Editor might not open and MATLAB might incorrectly report errors. Example 1 - Variable Editor Issues: >> for a=1:5 % Trying to open the Variable Editor now results in problems   a   end Example 2 - Variable Editor Issues: >>a = ... % Trying to open the Variable Editor now results in problems2
Workaround: After the keyboard function, put subsequent statements on a single line, separating statements using semicolons (;) as needed.Example 1 - Keyboard Function Workaround:>> keyboardK>> for a=1:5; a; endExample 2 - Keyboard Function Workaround:>> keyboardK>> a = 2As a workaround for the Variable Editor problem, do not try to open the Variable Editor during the execution of multiple-line statements. Instead, execute the statement and then open the Variable Editor.
